<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
green_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
red_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'R')
white_total = sum(n['pixels'] for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W')
print(f"Green: {green_total}, Red: {red_total}, White: {white_total}")

print("\nAll non-background nodes:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] != 'S':
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary'][:6]}")

# Check ASCII
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Current state ===")
for i in range(28, 52):
    print(f"Row {i}: {ascii_lines[i][35:55]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, pressing SPACE a second time changed the board again. The white shape has rotated or transformed. Now I see:
- Red blocks at rows 29-31 (top) and row 35-37 (left side)
- Green blocks at rows 29-31 (center top), rows 41-43 (inside white shape), rows 47-49 (bottom bar)
- White shape at rows 32-46
The SPACE action seems to rotate or transform the white shape. Each press changes the configuration. Let me think about what's happening:
1. First SPACE: moved white shape right, merged red blocks into a bar, revealed more green
2. Second SPACE: transformed the white shape again, now with a different orientation
Looking at the current white shape structure:
```
Row 32-34: WWW   WWW  (top section with gap)
Row 35-37: RRRWWW   WWW  (red block on left)
Row 38-40: WWWWWWW   WWWWWWW  (middle solid)
Row 41-43: WWWNNN   NNNWWW  (green blocks inside)
Row 44-46: WWWWWWWWWWWWWWW  (bottom solid)
```
The white shape looks like it's been rotated 180 degrees compared to the original! The original had the solid part at top and hollow at bottom, now it has hollow at top and solid at bottom.
Let me try pressing SPACE again to see if it rotates further or cycles back.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try SPACE again to see rotation pattern
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
